summary: Jeff Atwood shares several curated resources for learning about data visualization techniques. He highlights the Periodic Table of Visualization Methods, a Smashing Magazine collection of modern visualization approaches, and the Favourite Web Awards site for web-oriented design inspiration. The post serves as a brief reference guide for developers looking to improve their visualization skills before building rich internet applications.
key_points: The Periodic Table of Visualization Methods offers a comprehensive taxonomy of visualization techniques
Smashing Magazine's collection showcases modern, practical data visualization approaches
The FWA site catalogs creative web designs that push boundaries on visualization
Reviewing existing visualization techniques before building applications leads to better outcomes
money_quotes: If you're a student of Tufte like I am, you might find it helpful to review a sample of what visualizations and techniques are possible (or even advisable) before plowing ahead on your next Rich Internet Application.
key_insight: Before building data-rich applications, developers should study existing visualization catalogs to understand what techniques are possible and appropriate.
